inputPane.label: Input
inputPane.subhead: Give us a URL and a JSON <action>action sequence</action>, and we'll run it step by step.

inspectPane.label: Inspect
inspectPane.emptyDescription: Set up a sequence on the left and hit run. You'll get a step-by-step timeline, a screenshot at every stop, and a clear answer if something breaks.

actionEditor.fixErrors: Fix the issues above to run

happyPath.name: The happy path: fill a form
happyPath.description: Open a form, fill in a field, and submit. Everything should sail through, green all the way.

brokenSelector.name: A broken selector
brokenSelector.description: The same flow, but one step points at an element that isn't there. Watch exactly where it breaks — and why.

multiStep.name: A multi-step journey
multiStep.description: Hop across a chain of pages, grabbing a screenshot at each stop, so you can see how state carries from one action to the next.

runningIndicator.heading: Running your sequence…
stages: Snapping a screenshot at each step
Reading the page and pulling out content

timeline.heading: Timeline
timeline.failedBanner: Hit a snag at step {failed} of {total}
timeline.emptyBannerDetail: Every action ran to completion, but there were no screenshots or data to show for it.
timeline.noStepsDetail: Add a few actions and run to see the timeline.
timeline.apiTimeoutDetail: API-level timeout (60s) — the whole scrape call ran out of time. Try trimming a few actions or pointing at a page that loads faster.
timeline.waitTimeoutDetail: Step-level timeout — try giving this step a few more milliseconds to settle.
timeline.mismatchWarning: The screenshot counts don't quite line up — a few may be missing.

advice.selector_not_found: We couldn't find that element on the page. Double-check the selector, see whether the element only shows up under certain conditions, or confirm the page layout hasn't changed.
advice.wait_timeout: A wait or action on this step ran out of time. Try giving it a few more milliseconds to settle.
advice.navigation_timeout: The page took too long to load. Make sure the URL is right and that it loads within a reasonable window.
advice.api_timeout: The whole scrape reached the 60-second limit. Try trimming a few actions, or point it at a page that loads faster.
advice.rate_limited: Firecrawl is asking you to slow down for a moment. Give it a little time before trying again, or check the limits on your plan.
advice.blocked: Something blocked the request — most likely a CAPTCHA or bot protection on the site.
advice.empty_result: The action finished, but nothing came back. The content you're after may be missing, hidden, or loaded in a different way.
advice.unknown: Something unexpected happened. The raw error below should have the details.

lastGoodScreenshot.label: The moment before it broke
lastGoodScreenshot.caption: Here's how the page looked just before the action that failed.
lastGoodScreenshot.alt: Page state just before the failing step

network.title: We couldn't reach Firecrawl
network.hint: Looks like a connection hiccup — check your network, or see whether Firecrawl is having a moment.

api.title: Firecrawl ran into an error
api.hint: Your request made it through, but the API sent back an error.

validation.urlEmpty: Add a URL to get started
validation.urlInvalid: That doesn't look like a valid URL — try something like https://example.com
validation.actionsNotArray: Actions need to be a JSON array, wrapped in [ ]
validation.actionsEmpty: Add at least one action to run
validation.actionInvalidType: Action {n} has an unknown type "{type}". Try one of: {types}

warnings.heading: Heads up ({count})
warnings.screenshot_count_mismatch: We expected {expected} screenshots but got {actual}. A few may be missing or doubled up.
warnings.partial_results: We couldn't reach Firecrawl, so this timeline is our best guess based on when the request went out. Screenshots and timing data aren't available this time.
warnings.empty_sequence: The sequence finished, but came back empty — no screenshots, no content. The page may have loaded without the elements you were expecting.
warnings.prefix_timing_mismatch: The replay took {prefixDuration} instead of the expected ~{expectedMs}, so the page state may differ a little from the original failure.

missingKey.message: We need a Firecrawl API key to run this. Pop yours into the form, or set one up on the server.

invalidKey.message: Firecrawl didn't accept that API key. Give it another look and make sure it's the right one.
